description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing human figure, likely male, with arms held close to the sides and hands resting on the thighs. The figure is rendered in a classical style, with a smooth, elongated body and minimal detailing on the face and clothing. The sculpture stands on a rectangular base, which provides stability and elevates the figure. The back of the sculpture features a vertical support extending from the base to the upper back, a common feature in ancient stone statuary for structural reinforcement. The surface appears to be made of a light-colored stone, possibly marble or limestone, with a matte, slightly rough finish. The overall form is solid and monolithic, with no visible separations or interactive components.
description: A classical-style stone sculpture of a standing human figure with arms at the sides, mounted on a rectangular base and supported by a vertical back pillar.
description_view_0: Side view showing the figure's left profile, with the arm and hand resting along the thigh and the vertical back support clearly visible.
description_view_1: Opposite side view displaying the right profile, highlighting the smooth contours of the torso and the base's rectangular shape.
description_view_2: Another side view emphasizing the curvature of the back and the integration of the support pillar with the figure's form.
description_view_3: Rear three-quarter view showing the back support running from the base to the upper back and the overall solidity of the sculpture.
attribute_location_context: No visible environmental context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation setting.

license_info.creator_display_name: Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East
